subject: Ecology
title: Bogs
description: A short lesson to help you apply Bogs.

hook.text: A bog is a waterlogged world of sphagnum moss, acid water, and almost no nutrients.

buildup.text: Bogs receive water only from rain—no streams flow in, so minerals never arrive.

discovery.text: Sphagnum moss acidifies the water to pH levels that most plants can't tolerate.

twist.text: Carnivorous plants like sundews and pitcher plants thrive here by eating insects for nutrients.

climax.text: Bogs preserve organic matter for millennia—2,000-year-old bog bodies have been found intact.

punchline.text: Too acid for bacteria. Perfect for preservation.

quiz.question: Why do carnivorous plants grow in bogs?
options: Soil lacks nutrients so they eat insects instead
